explore.goal: Cover the entire arena efficiently while staying safe
constraints: Focus on systematic exploration; avoid long dwell times in one spot.
Do not collide with obstacles and robots.
Prefer forward movement unless obstacle cues demand recovery.

wall_follow.goal: Follow the wall consistently while staying safe and avoiding oscillations.
constraints: Interpret 'wall on left' as obstacle_left while obstacle_front is false.
Prefer actions that keep obstacle_left active; if obstacle_left disappears, rotate left to re-acquire it.
If obstacle_front occurs, perform a right turn to avoid collision, then re-acquire the left wall.
Avoid oscillation: never allow both rotate directions in the same state; keep a consistent turn direction until path_clear.
Use forward motion as the default only when path_clear and obstacle_left are true (wall present and front clear).

zigzag.goal: Explore the arena in a zigzag pattern while staying safe and avoiding oscillations.
constraints: Alternate between forward movement and alternating turn directions to create a zigzag path.
After each forward commit, prefer a turn in the opposite direction from the last turn.
If obstacle_front occurs, perform a recovery turn and resume the zigzag pattern.
Avoid oscillation: do not allow both rotate directions in the same state; commit to a turn direction until path_clear.

find_marker.goal: Find the marker object as soon as possible and get close to it and stop while staying safe.
constraints: Prioritize finding and approaching the marker quickly; exploration is only for reacquisition when marker is not visible.
Transition tuple format is strict: ("state", "event", "next"). The second field MUST be one of the allowed controllable/uncontrollable events.
Helper names such as forward_commit, rotate_commit_cw, rotate_commit_ccw, scan_full, marker_track are state names only; never place helper names in the event field.
When marker_seen occurs in clear/scan/search states, switch to marker tracking where move_to_marker is the primary controllable action.
Do not use stop as the primary controllable action when only marker_seen is true; continue approach until marker_close.
When marker_lost occurs during tracking, return to search/scan behavior instead of continuing move_to_marker blindly.
When marker_close occurs in any state, transition to a terminal goal state where stop is the only controllable action.
In search mode, alternate short forward progress with scan/rotate actions; avoid long straight runs without scanning.
In clear, do not force move_forward only; allow rotate/full_rotate or commit-based scanning to improve marker reacquisition.
After obstacle recovery, pass through a scan/search state before resuming forward exploration.
